Quantum Neural Architecture Search (QNAS) is a conceptual framework bridging quantum computing principles with automated machine learning. Unlike traditional backpropagation networks, QNAS relies on state superposition to explore multiple model topologies simultaneously.
The key advantage of QNAS over standard Evolutionary Algorithms is that it doesn't require iterative generation loops; instead, the topological search space collapses into an optimal state when evaluated against the validation loss function.
However, a major drawback is the requirement for low-noise qubits, which currently makes QNAS theoretical rather than practical.
